calls.content:
### Work Breakdown Structure
- Site Preparation
  - Site fencing and signage (2 crew days)
  - Survey and layout (2 crew days)
  - Minor grading and leveling (3 crew days)
- Foundations
  - Excavation (3 crew days)
  - Formwork and reinforcement (4 crew days)
  - Concrete pour and curing (4 crew days)
- Structural Shell
  - External and internal wall construction (8 crew days)
  - Roof framing and installation (6 crew days)
  - Roof waterproofing and insulation (3 crew days)
- Building Envelope
  - Window and door installation (3 crew days)
  - Exterior cladding/finishes (4 crew days)
- Interior Construction
  - Partition walls (3 crew days)
  - Plastering/drywall (4 crew days)
  - Flooring installation (4 crew days)
  - Ceiling installation (3 crew days)
- MEP (Mechanical, Electrical, Plumbing)
  - Plumbing rough-in and fixtures (4 crew days)
  - Electrical rough-in and fixtures (4 crew days)
  - HVAC installation (3 crew days)
- Finishes
  - Painting and decorating (4 crew days)
  - Millwork and shelving installation (3 crew days)
  - Hardware and accessories (2 crew days)
- External Works
  - Landscaping (3 crew days)
  - Pathways and parking (3 crew days)
- Finalization
  - Cleaning and commissioning (2 crew days)
  - Punch list and client handover (2 crew days)

### Total Estimate
77 crew days

### Assumptions
- Crew day = 1 standard crew (4-6 workers) working a full day.
- All materials, equipment, and approved design drawings are available at project start.
- No major weather delays or site access issues.
- Subcontractors are scheduled efficiently and work overlaps where possible.
- Library is a single storey, slab-on-grade, conventional construction.
- No unusual site conditions or environmental remediation required.
- Standard finishes and fixtures for a public library.
- Landscaping is basic (grass, shrubs, minimal hardscape).
- Parking and pathways are modest, suitable for a small neighborhood library.
